Name: Log4Shell Remote Code Execution
Synopsis: A remote code execution vulnerability exists in Apache Log4j2.
Description: The JNDI features used in Log4j2 do not protect against attacker-controlled LDAP servers. An attacker who can control log messages can execute arbitrary code.
Solution: Upgrade Apache Log4j2 to version 2.15.0 or later.

Name: Spring4Shell Remote Code Execution
Synopsis: A Spring MVC or Spring WebFlux application running on JDK 9+ may be vulnerable to RCE.
Description: Attackers can exploit ClassLoader access to achieve RCE via data binding.

Name: PrintNightmare - Windows Print Spooler RCE
Synopsis: The Windows Print Spooler service improperly performs privileged file operations.
Description: An authenticated attacker could exploit this to run arbitrary code with SYSTEM privileges.
Solution: Apply Microsoft security update KB5004945 or disable Print Spooler.

Name: F5 BIG-IP iControl REST Auth Bypass
Synopsis: An authentication bypass vulnerability exists in F5 BIG-IP iControl REST.
Description: Undisclosed requests may bypass authentication allowing an attacker to execute arbitrary system commands.
Solution: Upgrade BIG-IP to a fixed version or apply F5 mitigations.

Name: Pulse Secure VPN Arbitrary File Disclosure
Synopsis: Pulse Secure VPN allows unauthenticated remote attackers to read arbitrary files.
Description: The vulnerability allows attackers to steal credentials and session tokens.
Solution: Upgrade to Pulse Connect Secure 9.0R3.4 or later.

Name: SMBGhost - Windows SMBv3 RCE
Synopsis: A remote code execution vulnerability exists in the way SMBv3 handles requests.
Description: An unauthenticated attacker could exploit this to execute code on the target server.

Name: SSH Weak Algorithms Supported
Synopsis: The SSH server supports weak encryption algorithms.
Description: Use of weak algorithms may allow an attacker to decrypt traffic.
Solution: Disable weak algorithms in the SSH server configuration.

Name: SSL Certificate Signed Using Weak Hash Algorithm
Synopsis: The SSL certificate was signed using a weak hash algorithm (MD5/SHA1).
Description: Certificates signed with weak hashes can be forged.
Solution: Replace the certificate with one signed using SHA-256 or stronger.

Name: HTTP TRACE Method Enabled
Synopsis: The web server supports the TRACE method.
Description: TRACE can be used in cross-site tracing (XST) attacks.
Solution: Disable the TRACE method in the web server configuration.

Name: SMTP Service EXPN Command Enabled
Synopsis: The SMTP server allows user enumeration via EXPN.
Description: Attackers can enumerate valid email addresses.
Solution: Disable the EXPN command in the SMTP server configuration.
